steps.title: Solve the equation
steps.content: Split the absolute value: $$x+45=\pm 48.$$ This gives $x=3$ or $x=-93$.

steps.title: Identify the positive solution
steps.content: Take the positive value, $x=3$. The answer is (A) $3$.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. The given absolute value equation can be rewritten as two linear equations: $x+45=48$ and $-\left(x+45\right)=48$, or $x+45=-48$. Subtracting $45$ from both sides of the equation $x+45=48$ yields $x=3$. Subtracting $45$ from both sides of the equation $x+45=-48$ yields $x=-93$. Thus, the given equation has two possible solutions, $3$ and $-93$. Therefore, the positive solution to the given equation is $3$.
